Subject: Problem with Digital Marketing Services
Body: Dear Support, our agency was overcharged for digital marketing services last month. We suspect this might be due to an incorrect billing entry. We have already initiated a review of the invoices and reached out to support for clarification. We would greatly appreciate it if you could provide a detailed explanation of the overcharge and any error found. Additionally, please let us know the steps you will take to rectify this and ensure accurate billing in the future. We look forward to your prompt response. Thank you.
text:
Problem with Digital Marketing Services

Dear Support, our agency was overcharged for digital marketing services last month. We suspect this might be due to an incorrect billing entry. We have already initiated a review of the invoices and reached out to support for clarification. We would greatly appreciate it if you could provide a detailed explanation of the overcharge and any error found. Additionally, please let us know the steps you will take to rectify this and ensure accurate billing in the future. We look forward to your prompt response. Thank you.

Subject: Inquiry into SaaS Project Management Tool Features
Body: I am contacting you to seek information regarding the collaborative features of your SaaS project management tool, including real-time messaging, task delegation, and progress monitoring capabilities. Could you also provide details about any customizable workflow options or the availability of integrations with other productivity software? My aim is to find a tool that enhances my team's productivity and efficiency. Please supply more details about your tool's offerings and how they might assist my team.

Subject: With XRP Reserve Allocation You May be Eligible for 8-15% more Tokens
Body:
Exciting XRP Updates and Promotion – Don’t Miss Out!

Dear Community Member,

We’re thrilled to share that our latest blog post is now live, highlighting key XRP Ledger developments in 2025 and the return of our Community Token Allocation Program. From significantly lowered account reserves to the introduction of RLUSD and a revitalized market outlook, there’s plenty of news to explore.

This year’s promotion is your chance to earn additional XRP by participating in the ecosystem. Whether you’re a longstanding supporter or a newcomer, we’ve designed the program to be more inclusive, flexible, and rewarding.

Read the full post

Thank you for your ongoing support,
Team Ripple

Subject: Companies are preparing workers to be laid off — by their own AI
Body: First Up Plus, one man’s long journey to carbon redemption ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ First Up By Ashley Okwuosa Good morning. A man was rushed to hospital on Friday evening after being stabbed at TTC’s Kennedy station. Here’s the latest. MUST READS Steve Russell/Toronto Star Health The death of an Ontario ER: what a cottage-country closure means for the future of emergency care 27,000 signatures, 1,200 lawn signs, and two massive highway signs later, Minden residents gathered to mourn the emergency department they frantically tried to save. For almost 30 years, the ED served as an anchor for the community; now, residents fear the worst. As local health boards and the provincial government struggle to fill shifts in smaller hospitals hit hard by post-COVID attrition, Kenyon Wallace reports on why some public-health activists believe Minden is a portent of things to come across the province. Ramon Ferriera illustration THE FUTURE OF WORK IBM says it will replace 8,000 jobs with AI — but it has a solution for displaced workers IBM’s announcement of Watsonx, a new artificial intelligence platform designed to make businesses more efficient, comes after CEO Arvind Krishna estimated AI technologies would lead to roughly 8,000 IBM job cuts over the next five years. However, IBM says its worldwide re-skilling program, SkillsBuild, will help prepare 30 million people across five continents — who work at IBM and elsewhere — for the rapidly evolving job economy before the era of AI fully ushers in. Alex Cyr reports on IBM’s plan to re-skill workers and why experts aren’t sure how well it will work. Richard Lautens/Toronto Star The race to city hall Inside the Toronto election campaign: Buzzkill at the Disco!, debate bingo and other things you might have missed this week Whether on signs, posters or even in person at community events, the smiling faces and bold-type names of mayoral candidates will be hard to escape leading up to the June 26 election. While there was a brief pause in campaigning on Thursday afternoon due to a security threat, this week on the campaign trail has been busy. From Josh Matlow’s surprise appearance at a concert in the Beaches to endorsements from politicians past and present, Alyshah Hasham breaks down everything you might have missed this week. Walk it Back Climate The Guilt Trip: A Canadian man’s long road to carbon redemption Starting in January, Craig Cohon, a former executive for Coca-Cola and Cirque du Soleil, travelled on foot from Trafalgar Square to Istanbul to draw attention to clean carbon removal, trekking 4,200 kilometres across Europe. As he approaches his 60th birthday, World Environment Day and the end of his journey, Cohon calls his impending arrival in Istanbul on Monday the “end of the beginning.” Here’s what he told Jeremy Nuttall about his “gruelling” journey and his plan to bring lesser known solutions into the public discourse to combat climate change. Andrew Francis Wallace/Toronto Star GTA Here’s everything you need to know about Toronto’s best beaches Sidewalks are getting more crowded, ice cream is melting faster and people are smiling bigger — it’s officially summer. The heat’s return also marks the beginning of lifeguard supervision on Saturday at most Toronto beaches, continuing daily from 10:30 a.m. to 7:30 p.m. Whether you’re looking to swim, kitesurf, or enjoy the fantastic views, the city’s myriad beaches offer something for everyone. First Up’s Manuela Vega breaks down the best beaches near downtown Toronto, from washrooms and water sports to eats and atmosphere. POV David Olive: Think your flight is too expensive? Get used to it — here’s why today’s high prices could eventually seem like a bargain. Emma Teitel: I once feared COVID. Now the potentially deadly contagion to avoid is the moral panic about queer people. Here’s how it forced us to cancel a Florida family vacation. Donovan Vincent: Without context, this photo published in the Star depicting anti-semitic imagery is dangerous. Heather Mallick: Olivia Chow has the courage to admit Toronto needs to hike taxes. Amir Barnea: Canada is cracking down on taxes — so why does our national pension plan pay less than two per cent? Ricardo Tranjan: We need to take sides in the tenants’ rent strike. Support THIS Karon Liu/Toronto Star During a summer camping trip in 2016, Star food reporter Karon Liu learned to embrace outdoor dining. More importantly, Karon learned that disconnecting digitally is one important way to savor Ontario’s notoriously short summers. Here’s how the Star’s Fresh Air Fund supports students in developing lasting friendships and building memories that will outlive an internet trend. Thanks for reading. You can reach the First Up team at firstup@thestar.ca, and I will see you back here tomorrow. If you're not enjoying these emails, please tell us how we can make them better by emailing newsletterfeedback@thestar.ca. Or, if you'd prefer, you can unsubscribe from this newsletter by clicking the first link below. Prezado(a) Contribuinte,

Informamos que seu CPF encontra-se em situação IRREGULAR. Devido à não declaração e ao não pagamento de seus impostos, essa pendência gera complicações graves, como bloqueio de contas bancárias, dificuldades na emissão de documentos oficiais, como passaporte, abertura de contas, título de eleitor, entre outros, e o bloqueio em diversas transações administrativas. Além disso, a chave PIX também será suspensa até a regularização da situação.

Situação: IRREGULAR

Prazo para Regularização: IMEDIATO

Para regularizar sua situação, é necessário o pagamento imediato da multa sobre os valores não declarados. Atenção: Se a regularização não for efetuada, o seu CPF será SUSPENSO e quaisquer contas bancárias vinculadas a ele também poderão ser bloqueadas.

REGULARIZAR AGORA

Após realizar o pagamento, o sistema levará até 48 horas para atualizar seu status. Evite novos custos ou complicações regularizando a situação no prazo.

ATENÇÃO: Este e-mail foi enviado automaticamente pelo sistema de notificações. Para mais informações, acesse o site oficial ou entre em contato com o atendimento ao cliente.

This has drawn the attention of a small group of European researchers who have recently published their astounding discoveries... They have found the nucleic acid which makes some immune to developing tinnitus and how everybody can activate it in a matter of months, possibly even weeks... This is fresh-out-of-the-box info that you won't find anywhere else but in their short documentary. How to stop weird sounds in 3 easy steps. Stay healthy,

Subject: Re: [spambayes-dev] spoof detector
Body: on Mon Jul 09 2007, skip-AT-pobox.com wrote: > Dave> I did eventually find the lookup_ip_cache option, but frankly the > Dave> results are disappointing. I would have expected one slow round > Dave> in my train-to-exhaustion regime and then all following rounds to > Dave> go very quickly, but that doesn't appear to be the case. > > It flies for me after the initial run. > > Dave> Aha! spambayes is relying on atexit to close the cache and write > Dave> it out to disk, and tte obviously goes many rounds without doing > Dave> that. > > It should still have its cache in memory before exiting. Only the first > round should be slow. How many messages are you training on? (I have about > 250 messages total at the moment.) I have about 850. It's possible that the cache is getting pruned, but I actually upped those numbers so it would be less likely. It's still quite slow. > Dave> Problem is, my ssh connection to the server always drops before > Dave> training completes, and I'm not sure why (my ssh connections seem > Dave> to time out). > > Do you have access to screen(1)? That should help you retain sessions for > long periods of time if you're suffering from timeouts. It's in /usr/bin on > my Mac. I imagine it's avaialble available on most Linux boxes. I have it or can get it. How would I use it in this case? Do I run screen on the local machine or on the server? -- Dave Abrahams Boost Consulting http://www.boost-consulting.com The Astoria Seminar ==> http://www.astoriaseminar.com _______________________________________________ spambayes-dev mailing list bsfegmjrr-hnq@python.org http://mail.python.org/mailman/listinfo/spambayes-dev
text:
Re: [spambayes-dev] spoof detector

on Mon Jul 09 2007, skip-AT-pobox.com wrote: > Dave> I did eventually find the lookup_ip_cache option, but frankly the > Dave> results are disappointing. I would have expected one slow round > Dave> in my train-to-exhaustion regime and then all following rounds to > Dave> go very quickly, but that doesn't appear to be the case. > > It flies for me after the initial run. > > Dave> Aha! spambayes is relying on atexit to close the cache and write > Dave> it out to disk, and tte obviously goes many rounds without doing > Dave> that. > > It should still have its cache in memory before exiting. Only the first > round should be slow. How many messages are you training on? (I have about > 250 messages total at the moment.) I have about 850. It's possible that the cache is getting pruned, but I actually upped those numbers so it would be less likely. It's still quite slow. > Dave> Problem is, my ssh connection to the server always drops before > Dave> training completes, and I'm not sure why (my ssh connections seem > Dave> to time out). > > Do you have access to screen(1)? That should help you retain sessions for > long periods of time if you're suffering from timeouts. It's in /usr/bin on > my Mac. I imagine it's avaialble available on most Linux boxes. I have it or can get it. How would I use it in this case? Do I run screen on the local machine or on the server? -- Dave Abrahams Boost Consulting http://www.boost-consulting.com The Astoria Seminar ==> http://www.astoriaseminar.com _______________________________________________ spambayes-dev mailing list bsfegmjrr-hnq@python.org http://mail.python.org/mailman/listinfo/spambayes-dev

Subject: Pssst... Joe Biden is actually having a pretty productive presidency
Body: A recent policy win is flying under the radar. Here’s why you need to know about it. ͏ ‌ ͏ ‌ ͏ ‌ ͏ ‌ ͏ ‌ ͏ ‌ ͏ ‌ ͏ ‌ ͏ ‌ ͏ ‌ ͏ ‌ ͏ ‌ ͏ ‌ ͏ ‌ ͏ ‌ ͏ ‌ ͏ ‌ ͏ ‌ ͏ ‌ ͏ ‌ ͏ ‌ ͏ ‌ ͏ ‌ ͏ ‌ ͏ ‌ ͏ ‌ ͏ ‌ ͏ ‌ ͏ ‌ ͏ ‌ ͏ ‌ ͏ ‌ ͏ ‌ ͏ ‌ ͏ ‌ ͏ ‌ ͏ ‌ ͏ ‌ ͏ ‌ ͏ ‌ ͏ ‌ ͏ ‌ ͏ ‌ ͏ ‌ ͏ ‌ ͏ ‌ ͏ ‌ ͏ ‌ ͏ ‌ ͏ ‌ ͏ ‌ ͏ ‌ Joe Biden Is Having A More Productive Presidency Than You Probably Realize If you want to understand why the Biden administration gets so little credit for its accomplishments ― and why, perhaps, it deserves to get a little more ― pay attention to a little-noticed policy announcement from last Friday.The announcement was a list of 43 prescription drugs that are covered by Medicare and whose prices have risen faster than the rate of inflation. The list included relatively well-known drugs like Humira, which treats a variety of inflammatory conditions, plus some more obscure medications like Leukine, which helps cancer patients undergoing chemotherapy stave off infection.Until recently, such price hikes would have simply meant higher costs for the Medicare program ― and for individual seniors paying out of pocket. Or, to put it more bluntly, American taxpayers as well as American consumers were at the complete mercy of the drug industry’s pricing.But that’s not how things work anymore, thanks to the Inflation Reduction Act ― you know, the law that President Joe Biden and the Democratic Congress spent more than a year debating before finally enacting it in August 2022 and that almost everybody seems to have forgotten about since. Former President Donald Trump’s legal woes go beyond the dozens of state and federal charges against him — he’s having trouble finding qualified lawyers willing to help him wage his various courtroom battles. On Friday, attorney Jim Trusty submitted a request to withdraw from Trump’s defamation suit against CNN, citing “irreconcilable differences” with his client. Read More Tucker Carlson’s former producer at Fox News is out at the conservative network after a caption aired calling President Joe Biden a “wannabe dictator.” The employee, identified by the Daily Beast as former “Tucker Carlson Tonight” managing editor Alexander McCaskill, resigned, Carlson said on his Twitter show Thursday. Read More Two weeks after there was bipartisan relief that an economically disastrous government debt default had been avoided, there’s rising concern on Capitol Hill about whether the government can stay open past September. A government shutdown would shutter many parts of the government deemed nonessential, but keep intact some vital functions, like defense. Read More The Senate voted Thursday to confirm Nusrat Choudhury to a lifetime federal judgeship, putting the first-ever Muslim American woman on the federal bench. Choudhury, 47, is a longtime civil rights attorney with the American Civil Liberties Union. She worked for more than 11 years at the ACLU’s national chapter in New York City, and since 2020, has been the legal director for the ACLU of Illinois. Subject: Fogarty/NIH news: Reimagining global health; Delivering hope and health care in Haiti & Ghana; Improving heart failure care in India; DEI beyond borders
Body: Fogarty/NIH news: Reimagining global health; Delivering hope and health care in Haiti & Ghana; Improving heart failure care in India; DEI beyond borders May / June 2023 CUGH 2023: Reimagining Global Health Global Health emerged as an academic field over the last two decades. A plenary panel co-moderated by Acting Fogarty Director Dr. Peter Kilmarx addressed key overarching questions in the field. Full Article Delivering hope and health care in Haiti & Ghana By focusing on telemedicine and medication delivery during the overnight hours, MotoMeds aims to prevent illnesses from turning into emergencies. Full Article Improving heart failure care in Southern India During her Fogarty fellowship, Dr. Anubha Agarwal realized data collected in high-income countries wasn't going to work. Full Article Studying the effect of pesticide on Thai farmers and their families Pornpimol Kongtip of Mahidol University, Thailand, discusses her GEOHealth Hub project. Full Article Promoting diversity, equity, and inclusion beyond borders Training individuals from populations who are underrepresented as health researchers can help reduce health disparities around the world. Full Article Also in this issue Fellows & Scholars at 20: Paying it forward Facing the challenge of undiagnosed diabetes in Africa Journal highlights mental health of children & teens in Sub-Saharan Africa CUGH 2023: At the intersection of climate, nutrition & health CUGH 2023: Reducing the environmental impact of eye surgery CUGH 2023: Mental health, climate change, equitable collaboration People in the news: WHO gives lifetime achievement awards Gairdner honors Belizan McIff named deputy of HHS Global Affairs Belkaid to lead Pasteur Institute Senate confirms Global Women’s Ambassador NIH Clinical Center director Gallin retires Details: People in the news Global health briefs: NHGRI releases human "pangenome" USAID to support climate resilience WHO launches health inequality data repository G-FINDER publishes report on postpartum hemorrhage devices Academies publish proceedings on implementation science Details: Global health briefs Funding opportunities Chronic, Noncommunicable Diseases and Disorders Research TrainingApplication deadline: July 13, 2023 Global Infectious Diseases (GID)Application deadline: August 3, 2023 Fogarty HIV Research TrainingApplication deadline: August 22, 2023 Emerging Global LeaderApplication deadline: November 3, 2023 Global Brain Disorders ResearchApplication deadline: November 15, 2023 All Fogarty funding opportunities Subscribe to NIH global health funding opportunities email alerts Featured Event Pharmacy-Centered HIV Research: Current Landscape and Future Frontiers Date: June 28–29, 2023, at 1 p.m. ET/USALocation: Virtual This two-day event focuses on using implementation science research and collaboration between sectors to build models for effective pharmacy-based delivery and support of HIV-related services. Sponsored by the National Institute of Mental Health, the NIH Office of AIDS Research in partnership with Fogarty. Fogarty calendar of events Global Health Matters is produced by the Office of Communications at the National Institutes of Health's Fogarty International Center. For more information, please contact managing editor Judy Coan-Stevens at judith.coan-stevens@nih.gov or 301-496-7361. Permission to reprint Global Health Matters content: All text produced in this newsletter is in the public domain and may be reprinted. Please credit Fogarty International Center. Images must be cleared for use with the individual sources, as indicated. The Fogarty International Center, the international component of the U.S. National Institutes of Health, addresses global health challenges through innovative and collaborative research and training programs and supports and advances the NIH mission through international partnerships. Subscribe, update your subscription or unsubscribe to this e-newsletter from the Fogarty International Center subscription page. We encourage you to forward this e-newsletter to colleagues who may find it of interest. Subject: Hospital System Security Incident
Body:
Customer Support,
A security breach has been identified in the hospital's systems, leading to the exposure of sensitive medical data. This might be due to outdated software vulnerabilities.
Initial efforts to address the issue, such as updating software and enhancing firewall protection, have not resolved the problem.
The breach poses a risk to patient confidentiality and requires urgent assistance to rectify the situation.
We would appreciate detailed information on the outdated software and the firewall protection measures that have been implemented so far, and we are ready to schedule a call at to discuss the next steps and provide guidance on resolving the issue and preventing future breaches.

The breach poses a risk to patient confidentiality and requires urgent assistance to rectify the situation.
We would appreciate detailed information on the outdated software and the firewall protection measures that have been implemented so far, and we are ready to schedule a call at to discuss the next steps and provide guidance on resolving the issue and preventing future breaches.

Subject: Accounts maintenance and verifications
Body: Checking & Savings > Online Banking In attention of all Columbus Bank & Trust customers, As the Internet and information technology enable us to expand our services, we are committed to maintaining the trust customers have placed in us for protecting the privacy and security of information we have about you. In order to protect your information against unauthorized access, identity theft and account fraud we earnestly ask you to update your profile. To get started, please click the link below and login to your account: If you received this notice and you are not the authorized account holder, please be aware that it is in violation of our policy to represent oneself as another Columbus Bank & Trust user. Such action may also be in violation of local, national, and/or international law. Columbus Bank & Trust is committed to assist law enforcement with any inquiries related to attempts to misappropriate personal information with the intent to commit fraud or theft. Information will be provided at the request of law enforcement agencies to ensure that perpetrators are prosecuted to the fullest extent of the law. Thanks for your patience as we work together to protect your account. Regards, Columbus Bank & Trust Support Center.

Subject: Assistance Required with Investment Analytics Tools
Body: Respected Customer Support, I am contacting you to seek recommendations for analytics tools that can assist in investment optimization. As someone focused on making informed investment decisions, I believe possessing the right data is crucial. I've come across several tools, but I am uncertain which ones would be most suitable for my needs. Could you kindly suggest some analytics tools that would be beneficial for investment optimization? I would greatly appreciate any guidance you can offer on this topic. Furthermore, I would like to understand what specific features to look for when choosing an analytics tool. Are there any tools you recommend for beginners, and are there more advanced tools that would be appropriate for someone with more experience? I am eagerly awaiting your response and hope to learn more about the analytics tools you recommend. Thank you for your time and assistance.

Subject: Re: [SM-USERS] Autorespond plugin
Body: On 2/8/08, kenmiles wrote: > > I am using squirrelmail 1.4.13 with Dovecote and postfix on ubuntu 6.10. > Is there a way to have the autorespond plugin send a vacation message every > time an email is received from the same email address instead of just the > first time a message is sent. That's not a question for the plugin. The plugin merely sets the responder on/off and with a message. Your mail delivery system does the actual autoresponding. You need to know how to configure your autoresponse system. If you don't know how to do that, read up on your mail system and ask in the right forum. Also note that "Local User Autoresponder and Mail Forwarder" will also work for you, but the answer to your question will still be the same.
text:
Re: [SM-USERS] Autorespond plugin

On 2/8/08, kenmiles wrote: > > I am using squirrelmail 1.4.13 with Dovecote and postfix on ubuntu 6.10. > Is there a way to have the autorespond plugin send a vacation message every > time an email is received from the same email address instead of just the > first time a message is sent. That's not a question for the plugin. The plugin merely sets the responder on/off and with a message. Your mail delivery system does the actual autoresponding. You need to know how to configure your autoresponse system. If you don't know how to do that, read up on your mail system and ask in the right forum. Also note that "Local User Autoresponder and Mail Forwarder" will also work for you, but the answer to your question will still be the same.

Subject: System Security Incident
Body:
Customer Support,

I am writing to report a recent security incident involving the hospital's system. An unauthorized access attempt was detected, which may have been facilitated by weak password protocols. In addressing this issue, we have implemented stronger password policies and conducted a comprehensive system audit to identify potential vulnerabilities.

The incident occurred on [date] and was detected by the security monitoring system. Upon investigation, it was determined that the unauthorized access attempt was likely due to the use of a weak password.

Subject: Re: Urgent: Production environment inaccessible after IAM role change
Body:
Dear John,

Our engineers have identified the issue and applied a fix. You should now be able to access your production environment using the `prod-admin` role.

The problem stemmed from a caching issue following the role update. We apologize for the inconvenience caused by this outage.

We will conduct a thorough post-mortem to prevent similar incidents from occurring in the future.

If you face any further issues, please don't hesitate to contact us.

Sincerely,
Aetheros Support
text:

Subject: [zzzzteana] A New Theory on Mapping the New World
Body:
http://www.washingtonpost.com/wp-dyn/articles/A46455-2002Oct5.html

A New Theory on Mapping the New World

By Guy Gugliotta
Washington Post Staff Writer
Monday, October 7, 2002; Page A07

In 1507, a group of scholars working in France produced an extraordinary map
of the world, the first to put the still-recent discoveries of Columbus and
others into a new continent separate from Asia, and to call that continent
"America." With the Waldseemuller map, the New World was born.
But there was something else. What would later come to be called South
America and Central America were surprisingly well-shaped, not only on the
east coast, where explorers had already sailed, but also on the west coast
-- which no European was known to have seen.
The ice cream cone bulge that sticks out into the Pacific at the junction of
modern-day Chile and Peru is readily visible and in almost exactly the right
geographical spot -- not only in the main map, but also in an inset printed
along its top.
The shape of South America in the main map appears distorted because of the
curvature of the Earth.
It is an improbable coincidence, if it was a coincidence, for the map -- 12
large printed pages to be arrayed in one 36-square-foot wall display -- was
published six years before Vasco Balboa's 1513 trip across the Isthmus of
Panama and 12 years before Ferdinand Magellan's 1519-22 trip around the
world.
Did someone get there earlier?
...
